groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Post type, project context, readers, post goal, central story, relationship, stage, language, and desired depth.

groups.title: Story & Evidence
groups.description: Founder background, timeline, challenges, lessons, metrics, and references that make the post concrete and credible.

groups.title: Voice & Community Fit
groups.description: Tone, transparency, title direction, formatting, and the question used to invite useful founder discussion.

groups.title: Promotion & Safeguards
groups.description: Promotion level, optional project link, and final restrictions that prevent disguised advertising or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Post type
variables.placeholder: Select the kind of post
variables.hint: Choose the structure that best matches what you want to share.

help.what: Provide the post type before generating the Indie Hackers post.
help.why: Different founder stories require materially different structures and closing questions.
help.example: Good example: Build update
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, generic startup stories, fake vulnerability, fake success metrics, or changing the post into an ad.

variables.label: Project or topic
variables.placeholder: Example: Creatornivo, a content-template platform
variables.hint: Enter the project name or the specific founder topic being discussed.

help.what: Provide the project or topic before generating the Indie Hackers post.
help.why: The model needs a clear subject around which to build the post.
help.example: Good example: Creatornivo, a content-template platform
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, generic startup stories, fake vulnerability, fake success metrics, or changing the post into an ad.

variables.label: What are you building?
variables.placeholder: Describe what it does, who it helps, and its current state.
variables.hint: Give readers enough context to understand the project without opening a link.

help.what: Provide the what are you building? before generating the Indie Hackers post.
help.why: The project’s function and value cannot be safely inferred from its name alone.
help.example: Good example: A structured AI workspace that helps creators turn briefs into platform-ready content.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, generic startup stories, fake vulnerability, fake success metrics, or changing the post into an ad.

variables.label: Goal of this post
variables.placeholder: Select the main outcome
variables.hint: Choose one primary goal so the post remains focused.

help.what: Provide the goal of this post before generating the Indie Hackers post.
help.why: The goal determines what information receives emphasis and how the post closes.
help.example: Good example: Share useful progress
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, generic startup stories, fake vulnerability, fake success metrics, or changing the post into an ad.

variables.label: Who should read it?
variables.placeholder: Example: solo SaaS founders struggling to validate an early product
variables.hint: Describe the builders most likely to benefit or provide useful feedback.

help.what: Provide the who should read it? before generating the Indie Hackers post.
help.why: Audience context changes terminology, examples, depth, and the discussion question.
help.example: Good example: Solo SaaS founders struggling to validate an early product without fake launch hype.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, generic startup stories, fake vulnerability, fake success metrics, or changing the post into an ad.

variables.label: Main story or message
variables.placeholder: Explain what happened, what changed, or what you want to discuss.
variables.hint: Focus on the specific event, decision, realization, or question behind the post.

help.what: Provide the main story or message before generating the Indie Hackers post.
help.why: This is the unique factual and narrative foundation of the post.
help.example: Good example: We replaced generic prompts with structured template forms and learned that field quality matters more than template count.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, generic startup stories, fake vulnerability, fake success metrics, or changing the post into an ad.

variables.label: Your relationship
variables.placeholder: Select your relationship
variables.hint: Used to present your connection transparently and avoid disguised promotion.

help.what: Optional context for the your relationship field.
help.why: Community posts must not misrepresent a commercial or professional affiliation.
help.example: Good example: Founder or builder
help.avoid: Avoid disguising commercial relationships, pretending independence, link dumping, or asking for votes, signups, praise, or traffic.

variables.label: Current stage
variables.placeholder: Select the current stage
variables.hint: Helps frame expectations, evidence, and next steps realistically.

help.what: Optional context for the current stage field.
help.why: Stage controls proof, limitations, and what kind of feedback is appropriate.
help.example: Good example: Private beta
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Post length
variables.placeholder: Select the preferred depth
variables.hint: Auto lets the model choose the length that best fits the story.

help.what: Optional context for the post length field.
help.why: Length affects how much context, evidence, and explanation should appear.
help.example: Good example: Medium
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Select output language
variables.hint: Generate the post package in the selected language.

help.what: Optional context for the output language field.
help.why: The output language should match the intended community audience.
help.example: Good example: English
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Relevant background
variables.placeholder: Share only background that helps readers understand the decision or lesson.
variables.hint: Leave blank when background is not relevant.

help.what: Optional context for the relevant background field.
help.why: Background can add credibility when it explains context without becoming a biography.
help.example: Good example: Solo founder building a content workflow product while validating with a small beta audience.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Context and timeline
variables.placeholder: Add key dates, sequence of events, or what happened before and after.
variables.hint: Use approximate timing if exact dates are not important or not confirmed.

help.what: Optional context for the context and timeline field.
help.why: Timeline helps readers understand cause, order, and tradeoffs.
help.example: Good example: Started with a simple generator, audited weak templates, and migrated the first batch over two weeks.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Challenges and mistakes
variables.placeholder: Describe constraints, wrong assumptions, failed attempts, or unresolved issues.
variables.hint: Be specific without dramatizing or blaming others.

help.what: Optional context for the challenges and mistakes field.
help.why: Honest challenges make the post useful instead of promotional.
help.example: Good example: We almost shipped fake-looking social proof and too many vague template fields.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Lessons learned
variables.placeholder: List the practical takeaways other founders can apply.
variables.hint: Focus on transferable learning rather than generic motivation.

help.what: Optional context for the lessons learned field.
help.why: Lessons are what make the post valuable even without clicking a product link.
help.example: Good example: Prompt quality is only useful when every variable has a clear field and examples.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Metrics or evidence
variables.placeholder: Add only confirmed metrics, results, screenshots, quotes, or evidence.
variables.hint: Include definitions, timeframe, denominator, and uncertainty when relevant.

help.what: Optional context for the metrics or evidence field.
help.why: Metrics and evidence strengthen the post only when they are accurate and contextualized.
help.example: Good example: Use only confirmed metrics here, such as 15 migrated templates or 5 daily free generations.
help.avoid: Avoid invented metrics, customer quotes, sources, screenshots, links, benchmarks, or unsupported validation.

variables.label: Sources or references
variables.placeholder: Paste approved links, references, posts, docs, or public sources.
variables.hint: Do not include private or unverified sources.

help.what: Optional context for the sources or references field.
help.why: Source links can support claims, but the model must not invent them.
help.example: Good example: Approved changelog, public post, or product page: https://www.creatornivo.com
help.avoid: Avoid invented metrics, customer quotes, sources, screenshots, links, benchmarks, or unsupported validation.

variables.label: Writing tone
variables.placeholder: Select the writing tone
variables.hint: Choose a tone that feels natural for a founder-community post.

help.what: Optional context for the writing tone field.
help.why: Tone controls whether the post reads as candid, analytical, reflective, or conversational.
help.example: Good example: Clear and candid
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Transparency level
variables.placeholder: Select transparency depth
variables.hint: Choose how openly the post should discuss limits, uncertainty, and setbacks.

help.what: Optional context for the transparency level field.
help.why: Indie Hackers posts work best when transparent without exposing private or unsafe details.
help.example: Good example: Balanced
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Title approach
variables.placeholder: Select title direction
variables.hint: Choose the type of title angle or let the model infer it.

help.what: Optional context for the title approach field.
help.why: Title direction changes whether the post leads with a result, lesson, question, or story.
help.example: Good example: Lesson-led
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Formatting preference
variables.placeholder: Choose formatting preferences; or list preferences separated by commas.
variables.hint: Select preferred structure, or leave Auto for a readable founder-community layout. Available options: Auto, Short paragraphs, Section headings, Bullet points, Chronological flow, Narrative-first.

help.what: Optional context for the formatting preference field.
help.why: Formatting affects scanability and whether the post uses headings, bullets, or narrative flow.
help.example: Good example: Short paragraphs, bullet points
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Question for readers
variables.placeholder: Add one specific question you want the community to answer.
variables.hint: Leave blank when no discussion question is needed.

help.what: Optional context for the question for readers field.
help.why: A focused question invites useful replies without engagement bait.
help.example: Good example: How do you decide when a feature is useful enough to ship without extra polish?
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.

variables.label: Project promotion
variables.placeholder: Select how much project promotion is appropriate
variables.hint: Control whether the post omits links, mentions the project, or asks for direct feedback.

help.what: Optional context for the project promotion field.
help.why: Promotion level prevents the post from becoming a disguised ad or link drop.
help.example: Good example: Mention only
help.avoid: Avoid disguising commercial relationships, pretending independence, link dumping, or asking for votes, signups, praise, or traffic.

variables.label: Project URL
variables.placeholder: https://www.creatornivo.com
variables.hint: Shown only when promotion level allows a link.

help.what: Optional context for the project url field.
help.why: A supplied URL can be included once without inventing or requesting a link.
help.example: Good example: https://www.creatornivo.com
help.avoid: Avoid invented, unrelated, affiliate, shortened, or misleading URLs.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add final restrictions, must-use wording, claims to avoid, or special context.
variables.hint: Use this for requirements not already covered by another field.

help.what: Optional context for the additional requirements field.
help.why: A controlled final field handles edge cases without adding unnecessary form inputs.
help.example: Good example: No fake MRR, users, quotes, launch results, or disguised promotion.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake traction, fake community demand, confidential details, manipulative CTAs, or unsupported founder claims.
